title: Standardized Clinical Protocol for 650 nm and 810 nm Diode Laser Photobiomodulation After Dental Implant Placement
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7qih","text":"This protocol describes a randomized controlled clinical procedure for evaluating the analgesic effects of photobiomodulation using 650 nm and 810 nm diode lasers following dental implant placement in the posterior maxilla. The procedure standardizes surgical parameters, laser irradiation settings, treatment schedules, and outcome assessments, including postoperative pain, analgesic intake, and oral health–related quality of life.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":107,"length":56},{"style":"bold","offset":370,"length":64}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7c2rk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ulh9","text":"Expected outcomes include reduced postoperative pain intensity and decreased analgesic consumption in active photobiomodulation groups compared with sham treatment. The protocol is designed to support reproducibility and transparent reporting of laser-assisted pain management following implant surgery.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"43k7m","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"88mjr","text":"Developed in accordance with SPIRIT guidelines for interventional protocols, this document underpins the randomized controlled trial “Analgesic outcomes of 650 nm versus 810 nm diode laser photobiomodulation after dental implant placement,” which has been accepted for publication in Scientific Reports. The study adheres to CONSORT recommendations and is registered at ClinicalTrials.gov (Identifier: NCT06988722). A preprint version is available on Research Square (DOI: 10.21203/rs.3.rs-6858207/v1).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":29,"length":17},{"style":"bold","offset":284,"length":19},{"style":"bold","offset":325,"length":7},{"style":"bold","offset":369,"length":57},{"style":"bold","offset":466,"length":36},{"style":"italic","offset":133,"length":107},{"style":"italic","offset":284,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines:
{"blocks":[{"key":"4skk5","text":"Laser Safety & Environment","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6qjua","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aoi6u","text":"Mandatory Eye Protection: All personnel (operator, assistant) and the participant must wear wavelength-specific safety goggles (OD > 5 for 650/810 nm) during all active and sham sessions to maintain safety and blinding.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":25},{"style":"italic","offset":169,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5dqt5","text":"Controlled Environment: Ensure the treatment room is enclosed, with reflective surfaces covered and a warning sign illuminated outside.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eq8o1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ecc0c","text":"Surgical Standardization","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f9d4c","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4097d","text":"Time Limit: The surgical phase (incision to suture) must be completed within 20–25 minutes. Cases exceeding this duration must be excluded to prevent surgical trauma from confounding pain outcomes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":11},{"style":"bold","offset":77,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2q429","text":"Implant Consistency: Use only the specified implant dimensions (3.5–4.0 mm x 10 mm) and ensure placement is in healed D3 posterior maxillary bone to standardize insertion torque outcomes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20},{"style":"bold","offset":111,"length":34}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"23rj8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9mb95","text":"Blinding & Sham Protocol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7m6d","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6na9f","text":"Sham Fidelity: For the Sham group, the device must be positioned identically (gentle contact) and the "beep" sound simulator must be synchronized exactly with the 60-second "treatment" to ensure the participant cannot distinguish the group assignment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"o5br","text":"Assessor Blinding: The outcome assessor and statistician must remain blinded to group allocation until the final database lock.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3fsju","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6n63f","text":"Data Collection & Pain Scoring","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":30}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1usbe","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3qv5","text":"Pain Definition: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9sv63","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fi7e","text":"Mild Pain (1–3): Noticeable but does not interfere with daily activities.
1 = Brief | 2 = Intermittent | 3 = Continuous","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16},{"style":"italic","offset":74,"length":9},{"style":"italic","offset":86,"length":16},{"style":"italic","offset":105,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"adnel","text":"Moderate Pain (4–6): Distracting; interferes with some activities but is manageable.
4 = Brief | 5 = Intermittent | 6 = Continuous","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20},{"style":"italic","offset":85,"length":9},{"style":"italic","offset":97,"length":16},{"style":"italic","offset":116,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b5bfr","text":"Severe Pain (7–9): Intense; limits physical activity/functioning and requires analgesics.
7 = Brief | 8 = Intermittent | 9 = Continuous","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18},{"style":"italic","offset":90,"length":9},{"style":"italic","offset":102,"length":16},{"style":"italic","offset":121,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f17no","text":"Unbearable Pain (10): Completely incapacitating.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7a3bf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f08ko","text":"Pain Scoring Logic:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3he3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"17tgr","text":"Two-Step Scoring System: Participants must be trained to determine their NRS score using the following hierarchical process to ensure the "worst pain" is accurately captured:
","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f3aa8","text":"Step 1: Identify the Worst Severity Category. Determine the highest intensity level felt since the last recording (Mild, Moderate, or Severe).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":45}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9h8rs","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"20eqk","text":"Step 2: Assign Score based on Frequency. Within that identified category, select the specific number based on how long that specific intensity lasted:
","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":40}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e1i4s","text":"Lower Score (1, 4, 7): Brief (transient, lasted a few minutes).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":2,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":22},{"style":"bold","offset":23,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6542r","text":"Middle Score (2, 5, 8): Intermittent (recurring but with pain-free gaps).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":2,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":23},{"style":"bold","offset":24,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"542oo","text":"Upper Score (3, 6, 9): Continuous (persistent throughout the interval).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":2,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":22},{"style":"bold","offset":23,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dvluo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f1jd3","text":"Scoring Example (for Participant Training):
","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":43}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"71n93","text":"Scenario: A participant feels "Moderate" pain for most of the day but experiences a single "Brief" episode of "Severe" pain.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":9},{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dpl33","text":"Correct Score: (7). (Logic: The "Severe" category takes precedence as the worst pain. Since it was "Brief," the score is the lowest in the severe range: 7).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":14},{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3s6e7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2ct18","text":"Rescue Medication: Acetaminophen (1000 mg) is permitted only if the score reaches the Moderate range or higher (NRS > 3). Any intake must be recorded immediately.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18},{"style":"bold","offset":56,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start:
{"blocks":[{"key":"oemd","text":"Ethics & Recruitment:
","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cbb02","text":"Obtain written informed consent.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ej2l4","text":"Assign a unique, anonymized Study ID to the participant.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"97fh2","text":"Confirm eligibility: Age 20–65, ASA I/II, sufficient bone volume, and absence of exclusion criteria (e.g., smoking >12 cigarettes/day).","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ohp9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"48dqv","text":"Patient Training (Crucial Step):
","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":32}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b7r11","text":"Demonstrate the use of the NRS pain scale and the analgesic log sheet before the surgery begins.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":70,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b3h50","text":"Verify the participant has a working method to access the electronic OHIP-14 form (e.g., WhatsApp/Email on a smartphone).","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5l9u9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"rs4h","text":"Equipment Calibration:
","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"56kro","text":"Group A (Sham): Test the sound simulator volume against the active device.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8tp3l","text":"Group B (650 nm): Verify power output is stable at 100 mW.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3gqmn","text":"Group C (810 nm): Verify power output is stable at 100 mW.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2vd8m","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3cvdo","text":"Surgical Prep:
","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5icem","text":"Prepare the surgical kit and ensure the specific implant size (ConicalFit, Nuvo) is available.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"41igc","text":"Confirm local anesthesia (Lidocaine 2% w/ Epi) and 5-0 silk sutures are ready.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4lrfu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a97r5","text":"Schedule Management:
","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c2ffo","text":"Map out the full treatment schedule (Days 0, 2, 4, etc.) on a calendar card for the participant to take home.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ksf3","text":"Set automated reminders for the participant's data entry points (6h, 12h, 24h).","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fn69l","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"bh4re","text":"Ocular Hazard: Direct or reflected laser exposure can cause permanent retinal damage. Never activate the laser without verifying that all persons in the room are wearing appropriate protective eyewear.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2fgti","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4vbea","text":"Thermal Injury: Although Low-Level Laser Therapy (LLLT/PBM) is non-thermal, a dirty fiber tip or prolonged stationary application at high power can cause tissue heating. Ensure the tip is clean and power does not exceed 100 mW.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"75c2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5lfpk","text":"Surgical Risks: This protocol involves invasive oral surgery. Standard risks include hemorrhage, infection, and nerve injury. Immediate clinical intervention takes precedence over protocol data collection if an adverse event occurs.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3r4ma","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ehj6v","text":"Data Validity: Failure to record pain scores at the correct time intervals (specifically the early 2, 6, 12-hour acute phase) significantly degrades the study's power. Emphasize compliance during the \"Before Start\" phase.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14},{"style":"bold","offset":168,"length":53}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Screening: Screen patients requiring single posterior maxillary implants. Verify eligibility (Age 20–65, ASA I/II, healed site, D3 bone quality).
Confirm participant identity using the assigned study identification code and verify group allocation envelope remains sealed prior to surgery.
Consent: Obtain written informed consent.

Allocation: Prepare sequentially numbered, opaque, sealed envelopes containing group assignments by an independent research assistant. Keep the allocation concealed in an opaque envelope until the moment of laser application.

Randomization: Use the computer-generated sequence to assign the participant (1:1:1) to Group A (810 nm), Group B (650 nm), or Group C (Sham). Stratify randomization by sex using a fixed block size of six.
Anonymization: assign a unique, anonymized Study ID to each participant.
All PBM sessions are performed by a single trained investigator to ensure consistent probe placement, power settings, and session timing.

Record the following for each PBM session: - Participant ID - Session number and date - Group allocation - Device and wavelength used - Time of session - Operator initials - Any deviations from standard procedure
Document any deviations from the planned measurement schedule, missing data, or participant non-compliance. Maintain logs for audit and reproducibility purposes.
Maintain a secure master dataset for record-keeping and reproducibility. Ensure that all statistical code and procedures are documented and reproducible.

Pain NRS record Training (Crucial): Train the participant on the Two-Step NRS Logic: Step 1: Identify severity category (Mild 1–3, Moderate 4–6, Severe 7–9). Step 2: Assign specific score based on frequency (Brief = lower score, Intermittent = middle score, Continuous = upper score). Example: "Continuous Moderate Pain" = Score 6.
Baseline Assessment: Administer the baseline OHIP-14 questionnaire via the electronic link (Google Forms).
Preparation: Administer local anesthesia (2% lidocaine with 1:100,000 epinephrine).
Incision: Start the timer. Raise a minimally invasive, full-thickness H-shaped flap to preserve papillae.
Osteotomy: Prepare the implant bed according to the manufacturer’s drill sequence for Nuvo ConicalFit implants.
Placement: Insert the implant (single two-piece 3.5–4.0 mm diameter, 10 mm length) subcrestally.
Critical Check: Ensure insertion torque is ≥ 35 Ncm. If stability is not achieved, exclude the patient.
Closure: Suture with interrupted 5-0 silk sutures. Stop the timer.

Time Check: Confirm the procedure duration was 20–25 minutes. Document the exact time and designate this time point as time zero for all postoperative pain, analgesic intake, and photobiomodulation scheduling.
Preparation: Operator, assistant, and participant must put on safety goggles (specific to the assigned wavelength, or opaque/dark for Sham blinding).
Device Setup: Group A (810 nm): Set to 100 mW, CW. Group B (650 nm): Set to 100 mW, CW. Group C (Sham): Prepare sound simulator.
Application: Place the 0.5 cm² probe in gentle contact with the tissue.

Irradiation Sequence: Treat three specific points for 60 seconds each: Point 1: Buccal aspect of the implant. Point 2: Palatal aspect of the implant. Point 3: Crestal aspect (directly over the suture line).
Total Dose: Ensure total time is 180 seconds (3 minutes) and total energy is 18 J (6 J per point).
Blinding Maintenance: For the Sham group, hold the probe in the exact same positions for 60 seconds per point while playing the laser emission sound.
Instruction: Instruct the patient to record NRS scores and analgesic intake at 2, 6, 12, 24, 48, and 72 hours. Remind them to take Acetaminophen (1000 mg) only if NRS > 3 (Moderate/Severe).
Follow-Up Sessions: Repeat the laser irradiation protocol (Steps 12–17) on Day 2 (48h), Day 4, Day 7, Day 9, and Day 11; for a total of 6 sessions over 2 weeks (3 sessions/week).
Suture Removal: Remove sutures on Day 7.
Final Assessment: Administer the post-implant OHIP-14 at 1 week and post-prosthesis OHIP-14 1 week after the final restoration visit.
Digitization: Transcribe NRS scores, analgesic logs, and OHIP-14 responses into the master database.
Verification: Check datasets for completeness. Address missing entries by consulting source documents. Document any "Last Observation Carried Forward" (LOCF) applications for missing pain scores.

Pain Analysis: Calculate daily maximum pain (0–24h, 24–48h, 48–72h). Analyze acute dynamics (2, 6, 12, 24h). Compare groups using ANOVA, or Linear Mixed Model analysis (or Kruskal–Wallis for non-parametric data).
Analgesic Analysis: Calculate the frequency of rescue medication use (Yes/No) per time point and compare using Chi-square tests.
Quality of Life Analysis: Compare mean OHIP-14 scores using paired t-tests (within groups) and ANOVA (between groups).
Reporting: Report data as Mean ± SD (or Median/IQR) with effect sizes (Cohen’s d/f). Frequencies and percentages for categorical outcomes. Significance is set at p < 0.05.
creator: Zein ZA Adham
